summary: The GD STI's EJ257 engine is powerful but has a well-documented Achilles heel: ringland failure. With proper tuning and maintenance, reliability improves significantly, but the risk is always present.

issues.name: Ringland Failure
issues.description: The EJ257's #4 cylinder piston ringland is prone to cracking under boost, especially on 93 octane with aggressive tuning. This is the most common EJ257 failure mode.

issues.name: Head Gasket Failure
issues.description: The EJ257 uses multi-layer steel head gaskets that can fail, causing coolant and oil mixing. Often accompanies or precedes ringland failure.

issues.name: Turbo Bearing Failure
issues.description: The factory VF39 turbo can develop shaft play and bearing failure, especially if oil changes are neglected. Oil starvation during cold starts accelerates wear.

issues.name: Throwout Bearing Noise
issues.description: The throwout bearing develops a chirping noise when the clutch pedal is released. Annoying but not immediately dangerous. Replacement requires transmission removal.

issues.name: Boost Leak / Vacuum Line Deterioration
issues.description: The numerous vacuum and boost lines deteriorate with age, causing boost leaks, rough idle, and inconsistent boost delivery.

buyingTips: Ask about tuning history — a poorly tuned EJ257 is far more likely to suffer ringland failure. Custom protuned cars fare better than OTS maps
Check for white smoke on cold start — this can indicate head gasket seepage
Oil change history is critical — the EJ257 needs quality synthetic oil every 3,000–4,000 miles. Neglect kills these engines
Listen for knock on a cold start and under boost — any audible knock is a serious concern
Budget for a timing belt service if not documented within the last 60,000 miles — the EJ257 is an interference engine
